producer_note: Founded 1981 by Ron and Diane Miller and Lillian Disney; estate farms 360 acres across six vineyards in five Napa AVAs (Stags Leap District, Yountville, Coombsville, Carneros, Napa Valley).
Founded 1921 in Castiglione Falletto by Lorenzo and Paolo Scavino; fourth-generation family farms 30+ ha across 20 vineyards including Cannubi and Rocche dell'Annunziata; aged in large cask.
Founded 1920 by Domenico Meroi in Buttrio; 19 ha organically farmed including Zitelle and Dominin Grand Cru sites; Paolo Meroi crafts native white varieties plus Merlot and Refosco.
Founded 1983 by Eugenio Campolmi and Cinzia Merli in Bolgheri; pioneers of Cabernet Franc and high-density planting; produces Paleo, Messorio, Scrio; organic since 2002.
Family growing wine since 1744; rebuilt postwar by Josef Leitz; Johannes Leitz took over in 1985 and grew estate to 40+ ha mainly Grand Cru sites on slopes of the Rüdesheimer Berg.
Margaux Deuxième Cru Classe (1855) dating to 1661; 51 ha planted to 61% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot; certified organic 2023; owned by Chanel since 1994.
Founded 1983 by Silvia Imparato with consultant Riccardo Cotarella in San Cipriano Picentino; flagship Colli di Salerno IGT blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Aglianico and Merlot; cult since 1995.

Founded 2004 by winemaker Maggie Harrison (formerly Sine Qua Non); sources Syrah from White Hawk (Santa Barbara), Bien Nacido (Santa Maria), Stolpman (Santa Ynez); fermented at Antica Terra in Oregon.
Founded 1824 in Casteldaccia near Palermo; estates Suor Marchesa (Riesi), Risignolo (Salemi), Vajasindi (Etna); flagship Duca Enrico Nero d'Avola first released 1984.
Founded 1999 by Michael Twelftree and Richard Mintz as a négociant in Barossa Valley; sources Shiraz from Barossa, McLaren Vale, Clare, Eden, Adelaide Hills, Heathcote.
Founded 1883 by Melchor Concha y Toro in Pirque, Maipo Valley; largest South American producer with 10,000+ ha across Chile, Argentina and USA; produces Maule Valley wines.
Family-owned since 1928 (founded by Vittorio De Bortoli in Riverina); operates wineries in Riverina, Hunter Valley, Yarra Valley, Rutherglen; flagship Noble One Botrytis Semillon since 1982.
Haut-Medoc Troisieme Cru Classe (1855) in Ludon; 72 ha planted 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Merlot; chateau dating to 1730s; owned by Frey family since 2000.
Founded 1979 on the Martinborough Terrace; one of the original five Martinborough wineries; small estate focused on Pinot Noir, Syrah, Chardonnay; sold to Charlie Zheng in 2021.
Founded 1950 by Marchand-Grillot family union; 12 ha across 50 parcels in Gevrey-Chambertin, Morey-Saint-Denis, Chambolle-Musigny, Gilly-les-Citeaux and Brochon; Grand Cru Ruchottes-Chambertin.
Founded 1996 with Amador County Zinfandel by Joel and Sarah Gott; Napa Valley-based but blends grapes from across California, Oregon and Washington; food-friendly wines.
Baglio del Cristo di Campobello founded 2000 by the Bonetta family in Campobello di Licata, Agrigento; 50 ha (30 vineyards) on chalky soils planted mainly to Nero d'Avola and Grillo.
Family business since 1918, refounded 1987 in Canale d'Alba; 35 ha in Roero focused on Arneis and Nebbiolo; key crus Renesio, Printi, Genestreto.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru estate in Saint-Sulpice-de-Faleyrens; 5 ha (70% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon); organic since 1960s, biodynamic certified Demeter since 2012.
Chateau de Premeaux is a 15-ha family estate run by Arnaud Pelletier since 2008; certified organic; vineyards span Nuits-Saint-Georges, Hautes Cotes de Nuits, Pommard and Meursault among others.
Founded 1760 in Reims by Francois Delamotte; owned by Lanson-BCC group since 2006; non-malolactic style; royal warrant holder; Le Clos Lanson in Reims.
Founded 1999 by Massimo Piccin in Castagneto Carducci; 16 ha at Le Badie and Le Lame; produces Volpolo (Bolgheri DOC) and Sapaio (Cabernet-based IGT).
Bodegas Baigorri inaugurated 2002 in Samaniego, Rioja Alavesa; gravity-flow design by architect Inaki Aspiazu; Certified B Corporation; flagship B70 wine.
Founded 1989 in Pak Chong, Nakhon Ratchasima; 400 ha estate at edge of Khao Yai National Park, 350-380m elevation; grows Shiraz, Tempranillo, Cabernet Sauvignon, Chenin Blanc, Colombard.
Founded 1997 by Charlie Crocker and winemaker Pam Starr in St. Helena; 85-acre sustainable estate vineyard farmed since 1870s; specialises in Bordeaux varieties, especially Cabernet Sauvignon.
Founded late 1960s by Eugenio Tinazzi in Cavaion Veronese; headquartered in Lazise; production facility in Sant'Ambrogio (Valpolicella) since 2015; also wineries in Puglia.
Pessac-Leognan estate in Leognan, owned by Perrin family since 1956; ~100 ha (42 white, 50 red); Grand Cru Classe de Graves for both white and red; history dating to the 12th century.
Boutique Marlborough winery founded by former Cloudy Bay winemaker Eveline Fraser; vineyards in Waihopai Valley; Sauvignon Blanc, Riesling, Pinot Gris, Gewurztraminer and Pinot Noir.
Domaine Nathalie & Gilles Fevre based in Fontenay-Pres-Chablis; ~50 ha including Grand Cru Les Preuses, 1er Cru Fourchaume and Mont de Milieu; lutte raisonnee, transitioning to organic.
Family-run winery since 1950 in San Floriano (San Pietro in Cariano); 11 ha across San Pietro, Negrar and Marano in Valpolicella Classico; produces Valpolicella, Ripasso, Amarone, Recioto.
Set up 1985 in Villie-Morgon; 22 ha mainly in Morgon (Cote du Py, Corcelette, 3.14) plus Fleurie; key 'Gang of Four' natural wine pioneer; whole-cluster, no chaptalization or filtration.
Saint-Julien Quatrieme Cru Classe (1855); 102 ha planted 68% Cabernet Sauvignon; named after Connetable Talbot; owned by Cordier family since 1917; Caillou Blanc is the only St-Julien white.
Founded 1843 in Illasi, Verona; produces Soave Classico, Pinot Grigio, Valpolicella, Ripasso and Amarone across multiple Veneto and Trentino zones.
Founded 1975 by Steve Girard Sr. and Jr.; tasting room in Yountville, winery in Calistoga; sources Cabernet Sauvignon, Petite Sirah and others from across Napa Valley; flagship Artistry blend.
Loacker Wine Estates (founded 1979 in South Tyrol) operates Tenuta Corte Pavone in Montalcino and Tenuta Valdifalco in Maremma in Tuscany; biodynamic and organic since 1980s.
Pauillac Cinquieme Cru Classe (1855); 41 ha planted 62% Cabernet Sauvignon; separated from Batailley in 1942; acquired by Cazes family (Lynch-Bages) in 2017; vineyard expanded by 22 ha.
Paul Jaboulet Aine founded 1834 in Tain l'Hermitage; 100+ ha across Hermitage, Crozes-Hermitage, Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Condrieu, Cornas; flagship Hermitage La Chapelle; Frey family since 2006.
Terrazas de los Andes founded 1996 by Moet Hennessy in Perdriel; 8 high-altitude vineyards (980-1630m) across Mendoza including Gualtallary; Cheval des Andes JV with Cheval Blanc since 1999.
Tenuta San Leonardo in the Lagarina Valley, Trentino; estate documented since 589 AD, owned by Guerrieri Gonzaga family; flagship Bordeaux-blend San Leonardo IGT Vigneti delle Dolomiti.
Founded 1892 in Canelli (Asti); 52 ha; UNESCO underground cellars; specialises in Barbera, Metodo Classico sparkling, Chardonnay and Moscato d'Asti/Canelli DOCG; ~450,000 bottles annually.
Created 2001 by Joe Wagner (Caymus family); vineyard-designated Pinot Noirs sourced from Santa Lucia Highlands (Las Alturas, Balade), Sonoma Coast (Dairyman), Santa Maria Valley (Clark & Telephone).
Founded 1966 by Adolphe and Irma Ostertag in Epfig; Andre Ostertag (now joined by son Arthur) farms 34 acres biodynamically since 1997, including Muenchberg Grand Cru.
Founded spring 2014 by Frederic and Chantal Lafarge (Domaine Michel Lafarge, Volnay); 6.29 ha mostly in Fleurie (Bel-Air, Clos Vernay, Joie du Palais) plus Chiroubles; biodynamic.
Pauillac Cinquieme Cru Classe (1855); 60 ha planted 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot; owned by Casteja family/Borie Manoux since 1919; aged 18 months in 60% new French oak.
Grower-producer with house dating to 1741; family in Bouzy since 1492; ~20 acres in Grand Cru villages of Bouzy and Ambonnay; Pinot Noir focused; run by Jean-Francois Clouet.
Founded 1930 by Roger Piffre de Vauban in Molina (Curico Valley); Roberto Echeverria Jr. winemaker since 2001; sources from Curico and Maipo; pioneer of Chilean natural wine since 2017.
Producing Sangiovese in Montalcino since 1976; 200 ha estate (37 under vine) at 480-620m, among the highest in Brunello area; certified organic; acquired by Atlas Invest in 2017.

Founded 1829 in Ay by Jacques Bollinger, Paul Renaudin and Athanase de Villermont; family-run; key vineyards across Ay, Avenay, Tauxieres, Verzenay; royal warrant since 1884; flagship R.D. and Grande Annee.
Founded 1992 by Moreno Petrini at 300m above Lucca; 40 ha (16 vines) on limestone/sandstone; Demeter biodynamic since 2002; flagship Sangiovese-Merlot-Syrah blend Tenuta di Valgiano DOC.
Drew Family Cellars founded 2000 by Jason and Molly Drew; 8-acre estate vineyard within Mendocino Ridge AVA; certified organic; Pinot Noir focused with Anderson Valley tasting room in Philo.
Founded 1964 by Peter Newton and Michael Stone in Calistoga, Napa Valley; iconic white Mykonos-style hilltop winery; owned by Treasury Wine Estates; aerial tram visitor experience.
Pian delle Querci, Pinti family winery in Montalcino; 50 ha estate, 8.5 ha vineyards (6 ha Brunello DOCG, 2 ha Rosso DOC); traditional Brunello aged in Slavonian oak.
Margaux Troisieme Cru Classe (1855) in Cantenac; 62.5 ha planted 65% Cabernet Sauvignon; Tudor-style chateau by John Lewis Brown; owned by Le Lous family since 2019.
Founded 1981 in Renwick by Rex Brooke-Taylor; 17 ha on Marlborough's Golden Mile; one of NZ's oldest Riesling vineyards; owned by Sogrape; produces Sauvignon Blanc, Riesling, Pinot Noir.
Founded 1979 in Rutherford; owned by Leonardini family since 1993; 140+ acres across eight vineyards in Rutherford, St. Helena, Oak Knoll, Sonoma; Napa Green certified.
Burgundy producer/negociant founded 1849, based in Savigny-les-Beaune; covers 14 appellations around Corton including Meursault, Santenay, Pouilly-Fuisse, Aloxe-Corton, Saint-Veran.
Founded 1836 in Bergheim, run by 7th-generation Georges Lorentz; 33 ha on Bergheim hillsides; Grand Crus Altenberg de Bergheim (12.8 ha) and Kanzlerberg (1.75 ha); fully organic since 2012.
Founded 2004 by Gianmarco Antonuzi and Clementine Bouveron in Gradoli, near Lago di Bolsena; ~14 ha biodynamic; native Procanico, Roscetto, Aleatico; cult natural wine producer.
Founded 1963 by Ceferino Carrion (Jean Leon) in Penedes; first Spanish single-varietal Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay; owned by Familia Torres since 1994; led by Mireia Torres Maczassek.

Bordeaux estate with 17 ha of clay-chalk soils on the high plateau adjacent to Puisseguin (Saint-Emilion satellite); blend of 60% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, 10% Cabernet Franc; Castillon Cotes de Bordeaux.
Colombini family property since 1700s (land in Montalcino since 1352); produces Brunello since 1892; 350 ha across Montalcino, Scansano and Chianti; flagship 100% Sangiovese Brunello.
Domaine Henri et Philippe Jouan founded 1962 in Morey-Saint-Denis; ~3 ha; flagship Morey-Saint-Denis 1er Cru Clos Sorbe and Grand Cru Clos Saint-Denis; managed today by Philippe Jouan.
Wine estate documented since 1211 above Oestrich-Winkel in Rheingau; 80 ha 100% Riesling, 21 ha classified VDP Grosse Lage; one of the world's oldest continuously operating wineries.
Founded 1996 in Napa Valley by John and Maggy Kongsgaard, fifth-generation Napa natives; flagship 'The Judge' Chardonnay from family land east of Napa; low-intervention winemaking.
Francis Boulard et Fille founded 2009 by Francis and Delphine Boulard; 3 ha across Massif Saint-Thierry, Mailly (Montagne de Reims) and Vallee de la Marne; biodynamic certified 2015.
Founded 1838 in Ay by William Deutz and Pierre-Hubert Geldermann; member of Grandes Marques; acquired by Rouzaud family (Louis Roederer) in 1983; production grew to 2.4M bottles by 2018.
Founded 1982 by Jess Jackson; flagship Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay; family-owned (Jackson Family Wines); estate vineyards across Sonoma, Mendocino, Lake, Monterey and Santa Barbara counties.
Founded 1970, the only cooperative winery in the Brunello area; 94 members farming ~385 acres across Montalcino at 350-2000 ft; 100% Sangiovese Brunello aged 30 months in Slavonian oak.
Saint-Estephe Cru Bourgeois Superieur on a 51 ha single block by the Gironde, near Chateau Montrose; 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, 10% Petit Verdot; owned by CA Grands Crus since 2004.
Napa Valley estate-style 100% Cabernet Sauvignon brand; aged ~48 months in new French oak; limited production label distributed in part via Asian retailers.
Founded 1989 by Josep Lluis Perez in Falset, Priorat; 22 ha organic across original farm and three single vineyards; flagship Clos Martinet; today led by Sara Perez.
Pomerol estate purchased 1985 by Catherine Pere-Verge; 10.1 ha planted 80% Merlot, 20% Cabernet Franc; only top 5 ha used for grand vin; Michel Rolland consultancy since 1988; owned by Henri Parent.

De Stefani family in Refrontolo since 1624, winery founded 1866 by Valeriano De Stefani; based in Fossalta di Piave; produces Piave DOC reds plus Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco; organic/biodynamic.
Estate in Andlau under Kreydenweiss family since 1850, biodynamic since 1989 (Demeter); 13.5 ha including Grand Crus Wiebelsberg, Moenchberg, Kastelberg; managed by son Antoine since 2007.
Beyer family in Eguisheim since 1580; modern house founded 1867 by Emile Beyer; 15 ha family vineyards plus 40 ha worked with growers; Grand Crus Eichberg and Pfersigberg; HVE certified.
Family estate in Chambolle-Musigny dating to early 19th century, run by 4th-gen Anne and Herve Sigaut since 1990; 7 ha primarily in Chambolle plus parcels in Morey-Saint-Denis; organic farming.
Brand created 2017 by Vintage Wine Estates with HBO; winemaker Bob Cabral; Chardonnay from Central Coast, Red Blend from Paso Robles, Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley.
Champagne Dumangin J. Fils founded 1884 by Hippolyte Dumangin in Chigny-les-Roses (Premier Cru, Montagne de Reims); 8 ha mostly around the village; now run by 5th-gen Gilles Dumangin.
Founded 1335 in Eitelsbach (Trier) on the Ruwer; in same family since 1811; 19 ha almost entirely Riesling on the Karthauserhofberg single vineyard; renowned for dry, age-worthy wines.
Founded 1971 by John and Marli Middleton in Coldstream, Yarra Valley; 40-acre single estate vineyard; flagship Quintet Bordeaux blend ranked Exceptional in Langton's Classification.
Estate in Coteaux Varois en Provence appellation with 43 ha of vineyards surrounded by 300 ha of forest; high-elevation Provence terroir; flagship Cinsault-Grenache-Syrah rose; HVE certified.
Founded 1997 in Milagros (Burgos), Ribera del Duero; 155 ha of Tempranillo (half estate, half partner growers); part of Freixenet/Henkell; produces Roble, Crianza, Reserva.
Founded 1999 by Dominique Lafon (Domaine des Comtes Lafon, Meursault) in Milly-Lamartine; 26 ha biodynamic across Maconnais appellations including Macon-Villages; head winemaker Caroline Gon.

Roccolo Grassi in Mezzane di Sotto, eastern Valpolicella; relaunched 1996 by Marco Sartori (with sister Francesca since 2003); 15 ha organic across Valpolicella and La Broia (Soave); single-vineyard wines.
Pessac-Leognan estate in Leognan with vines since c.1310, late-18th century chateau; bought 1965 by Andre Lurton (family-owned); 48 ha red plus white wines; chateau is a listed Historic Monument.
St. Urbans-Hof founded 1947 by Nikolaus Weis in Leiwen; run by 3rd-gen Nik Weis; ~45 ha along Mosel and Saar with five VDP.Grosse Lage sites; member of VDP since 2000.
Grower-producer in Le Mesnil-sur-Oger making Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs since 1919; 19+ ha Chardonnay across Le Mesnil, Oger, Avize and Cramant; flagship Cuvee de Reserve and Les Chetillons.
Natural-wine pioneer in Oslavia, Collio (Friuli); ~18 ha of eocene marl vines; Stanko Radikon adopted natural methods in 1995; led by son Sasa Radikon since 2016; iconic skin-contact orange wines.

Founded 1999 by Michael Twelftree and Richard Mintz; Adelaide Hills 'Harriet's Garden' Shiraz part of Garden Series sourcing from six Australian regions including Barossa, McLaren Vale, Clare, Eden, Adelaide Hills, Heathcote.
Founded 1584 in Ay by Pierre Gosset; oldest wine house in Champagne; relocated production to a 2-ha Domaine in Epernay in 2009; owned by Renaud-Cointreau group since 1994; non-malolactic style.
Pomerol estate of ~3 ha in two plots, planted 90% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc; recently acquired by Ronan Laborde of Chateau Clinet; recent vintages scored 93pts by Parker, Suckling, Dunnuck.
Family-owned Napa winery in the Oak Knoll District (Big Ranch Rd) plus tasting room in Larkspur; 50-acre estate plus fruit from trusted Napa Valley growers; small-batch Italian-influenced wines.
Revana Estate founded by Dr. Madaiah Revana, vineyard planted 1998; 9-acre estate in St. Helena planted to Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot; limited production sold mostly direct.

Founded 1850 (Duveau family); taken over by Thierry and Marie Germain in 1991; 28 ha across 61 plots in Saumur-Champigny and Saumur appellations; biodynamic since early 2000s; Cabernet Franc and Chenin Blanc.
